# Stories — Analytics improvements batch

Simple, high-level stories — most are "investigate, propose an approach, and implement." No Shortcut/Helpin metadata blocks (per current direction); team is shown by the title prefix and product area is **Analytics**.

---

# EPIC: Analytics Reports Architecture Improvement

**Description:** Our analytics reports are generated by rendering the report page and capturing it (via a headless-browser → PDF service). When the report data is slow to load, the capture happens too early — so exports show loading spinners/placeholders instead of the real charts — and long renders can time out or fail. This epic reworks how reports are generated so exports are reliable and always reflect fully-loaded data. Starting with a research story to define the new approach; implementation stories follow once the approach is agreed.

## Story 1 — [BE] Research & propose a new architecture for analytics reports
*(Epic: Analytics Reports Architecture Improvement)*

### Description:
As the analytics team, we want a researched, agreed plan to fix how analytics reports are generated, so exported reports always show fully-loaded data (no loading spinners or blank charts) and don't fail on slow/large reports. This story is **research only** — investigate the current report-generation flow and its failure modes, then propose an approach for the team to review.

### Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] The current report-generation flow and its problems are documented (early capture showing loading states, timeouts on long/large reports, any other failure modes found).
- [ ] At least 2 approaches are proposed to ensure reports capture only fully-loaded content reliably (e.g. a "ready" signal before capture, server-side rendering, async generation with notify-when-ready), with trade-offs.
- [ ] A recommended approach is selected and written up with rough scope and follow-up implementation stories outlined.
- [ ] The proposal notes impact on existing scheduled/exported reports and any infra changes.

### Impact on existing data / other products:
None directly (research). Findings affect the reports/export feature (web). No mobile/Chrome.

### Dependencies:
None. Outputs implementation stories under this epic.

### Global quality & compliance
- [ ] N/A for most — research story. Capture cross-product/report impact in the proposal.

### Where to look (light pointer):
`contentstudio-backend/app/Jobs/Analytics/GenerateReportJob.php` → `app/Services/Analytics/ReportsHelper.php` → `app/Services/Pdf/GotenbergService.php` (Gotenberg headless-Chromium URL→PDF).

---

# Standalone analytics stories

## Story 2 — [FE] Migrate the analytics module to TypeScript & TanStack Query

### Description:
As the frontend team, we want the analytics module migrated to TypeScript and TanStack Query, so the code is type-safe, consistent with our newer modules, and easier to maintain. This is a **behind-the-scenes refactor** — the analytics UI looks and behaves exactly the same for users.

### Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] The analytics module's components/composables are converted to TypeScript (`<script setup lang="ts">`, typed composables/stores).
- [ ] Data fetching uses TanStack Query consistently (replacing remaining ad-hoc/store-based fetching).
- [ ] No change to analytics features, layout, or behavior that a user would notice.
- [ ] Existing analytics tests pass (and types check) after the migration.

### Impact on existing data / other products:
No data or UX change. Web only.

### Dependencies:
None. (No design story needed — no visual change.)

### Global quality & compliance
- [ ] Mobile responsiveness — unchanged (refactor)
- [ ] Multilingual support — unchanged (no new copy)
- [ ] UI theming support — unchanged
- [ ] White-label domains impact review — unchanged
- [ ] Cross-product impact assessment — web only

### Where to look (light pointer):
`contentstudio-frontend/src/modules/analytics_v3/` (active module, mostly JS today) — and legacy `src/modules/analytics/`. Follow the TS + TanStack patterns already used in other v_ modules.

---

## Story 3 — [BE] Change the daily analytics sync to fetch only the current day's data

### Description:
As the analytics team, we want the daily data-sync job to fetch **only the current day's** data instead of re-fetching the last ~10–14 days on every run, so syncs are faster, lighter on platform API quotas, and cheaper to run — without losing data accuracy.

### Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] The daily sync fetches only the current day's data (instead of the current ~10–14 day look-back window).
- [ ] Historical data already stored is unaffected; ongoing daily data stays accurate and continuous (no gaps).
- [ ] A short note covers how late-arriving/back-dated platform corrections are handled (or explicitly accepted as out of scope), so we don't silently lose adjustments platforms make to recent days.
- [ ] Platform API usage for the daily sync measurably drops after the change.

### Impact on existing data / other products:
Changes ongoing sync volume only; no change to stored history. Affects all analytics platforms fed by the daily sync.

### Dependencies:
None.

### Where to look (light pointer):
`contentstudio-social-analytics-go/src/cmd/jobs/fetcher/*.go` — the look-back/date-range window for each fetch (dev to confirm the exact window location).

---

## Story 4 — [BE] Handle expired tokens in the immediate analytics sync (stop returning success)

### Description:
As a user who triggers an on-demand analytics sync, I want to be clearly told when my connected account's token has expired, instead of the sync silently reporting success while no data is actually fetched. Today the immediate sync can return success even when the platform token is expired, which hides the real problem.

### Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] When an immediate/manual analytics sync runs for an account whose platform token is expired/invalid, the sync does **not** report success.
- [ ] The expired/invalid-token state is detected and surfaced with a clear, specific result (so the UI/user can be told the account needs reconnecting).
- [ ] Genuinely successful syncs continue to report success as before.
- [ ] The expired-token outcome is distinguishable from other failures (so it can drive a "reconnect your account" message).

### Impact on existing data / other products:
No data-model change; corrects the response/outcome of the immediate sync. Affects any UI that triggers on-demand analytics sync.

### Dependencies:
None.

### Where to look (light pointer):
`contentstudio-social-analytics-go/src/api/immediate_work_apis.go` and the platform fetch path it calls — the expired-token case appears to be swallowed upstream and mapped to success (dev to pinpoint).

---

## Story 5 — [BE] Improve the analytics post-thumbnails architecture

### Description:
As a user viewing analytics, I want post thumbnails to load reliably, so my reports and analytics views don't show broken or missing images. Today thumbnails rely on platform CDN URLs that can expire, leaving broken previews; we want a more durable approach. This story is **investigate-and-propose, then implement** the improved thumbnail handling.

### Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] The current thumbnail flow and why thumbnails break (e.g. expiring platform CDN URLs) is documented.
- [ ] An improved approach is chosen (e.g. caching/storing thumbnails, a proxy, or refreshing expired URLs) with trade-offs noted (storage, cost, freshness).
- [ ] Thumbnails render reliably in analytics views and exported reports, including for older posts.
- [ ] Existing analytics views/reports continue to display thumbnails without regression.

### Impact on existing data / other products:
May add thumbnail storage/caching. Affects analytics views (web) and exported reports. No mobile/Chrome.

### Dependencies:
Loosely related to **[BE] Research & propose a new architecture for analytics reports** (reliable thumbnails help report exports) — not blocking.

### Where to look (light pointer):
`contentstudio-frontend/src/modules/analytics_v3/composables/useCompetitorHelper.js` (`getMediaLink`) and `contentstudio-backend/app/Repository/Utilities/MediaRepository.php` (`Media` model).

---

## Story 6 — [BE] Investigate & fix the Sentry errors coming from the analytics Go service

### Description:
As the engineering team, we want to clean up the errors the analytics Go service is reporting to Sentry — the dev has flagged that these shouldn't be happening — so Sentry reflects real issues and we're not blind to genuine problems hidden in the noise.

### Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] The recurring errors emitted to Sentry from the analytics Go service are reviewed and grouped by root cause.
- [ ] Each group is either fixed at the source or, where it's an expected/non-error condition, downgraded so it's no longer reported as an error.
- [ ] After the change, the analytics Go service's error volume in Sentry drops to a clean baseline (only genuine, actionable errors remain).
- [ ] A brief note records what was fixed vs. intentionally silenced and why.

### Impact on existing data / other products:
No data change; logging/error-handling and possibly bug fixes in the Go analytics pipeline.

### Dependencies:
None.

### Where to look (light pointer):
`contentstudio-social-analytics-go/src/logger/sentry_hook.go` (Sentry auto-captures all zerolog `Error`+ events) + the Sentry dashboard for the Go project to identify the top error groups.
